draft.introduction: Matthew’s version of the greatest commandment, in which a lawyer tests Jesus. Jesus combines Deuteronomy 6:5 and Leviticus 19:18, declaring that all the Law and Prophets hang on these two commands.

translation.text: “Teacher, which is the great commandment in the Law?” And he said to him, “You shall love the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your mind. This is the great and first commandment. And a second is like it: You shall love your neighbor as yourself. On these two commandments depend all the Law and the Prophets.”
translation.original_text: ἀγαπήσεις κύριον τὸν θεόν σου ἐν ὅλῃ τῇ καρδίᾳ σου

translator_notes.note: “On these two commandments depend all the Law and the Prophets” (ἐν ταύταις ταῖς δυσὶν ἐντολαῖς ὅλος ὁ νόμος κρέμαται) is unique to Matthew. The verb κρέμαται (“hangs”) presents the entire Torah as suspended from love—remove love, and everything falls.
